channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Amazon Says It’s ‘Too Early To Discuss’ Future 007 Titles
item.content:encoded:
Recently, Amazon provided an update regarding the future of James Bond video games, as it was suggested that new titles will be solely published by Amazon, not IO Interactive. This sentiment arrived following the standout success of 007 First Light, with the game selling over 1.5 million copies in just 24 hours; you’d imagine that this is just the beginning for the series.

Well, in the latest statement by Amazon Game Studios, it’s been deemed ‘too early to discuss future projects.’ Instead, IO will have more to share regarding 007 First Light in the near future: any further than that seems to be unknown.

What Does 007’s Future Look Like Under Amazon & MGM?

The new statement from Amazon was received by VGC, and it was in response to the notion that future 007 titles would be ‘done by MGM and, theoretically, by Amazon Game Studios.’

That sentiment created a concern that IO Interactive would lose out on the ability to create future 007 titles, as it seemed like the resounding notion was that Amazon were in control, and IO would instead take the back seat. This hit especially hard after the resounding success and impact First Light has had.

In response to this concern, Amazon responded with this:

Amazon MGM holds the rights to any future James Bond video games but it’s still too early to discuss future projects.

We have a great relationship with IO Interactive and are proud of what we’ve accomplished together on 007 First Light. Our partners at IO will reveal more about 007 First Light in the near future and we’re looking forward to sharing what’s next.

This new statement doesn’t paint much of a clearer picture, but it was made abundantly clear that, whilst it’s too early to discuss future projects in the Bond franchise, Amazon has a ‘great relationship with IO Interactive.’ It doesn’t seem like they’re desperate to cut IO off, and the likelihood is that Amazon will simply take over on the publishing of the game, with IO in charge of development.

Still, as Amazon said itself, it’s too early to paint a specific picture, but with the commercial and critical success of First Light, you’d imagine some important conversations are taking place regarding Bond’s future—after all, a sequel was supposedly dependent on public reception to First Light, and could that be any better amid GOTY shouts?

What do you hope to see for Bond’s future? Should Amazon just let IO get on with it themselves? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Subnautica 2’s first major update, as well as the 50% drop in PS5 sales in the UK.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: First Details For Subnautica 2’s Next Major Update Revealed
item.content:encoded:
First details for Subnautica 2‘s next major update have been revealed, as Unknown Worlds begins to pivot from rapid hotfixes to the highly anticipated 1.1 update.

A roadmap of sorts was released a few weeks back, but this update clarifies what will be added in the next major update, as a new explorable region and craftable vehicle are at the heart of the game’s future.

Subnautica 2 1.1 Update Details

Information surrounding the 1.1 update for Subnautica 2 was shared on the official Subnautica YouTube channel, with Design Lead Anthony Gallegos providing the news in a Dev Vlog.

Before update 1.1, there are a few improvements and additions coming to the game, including the highly requested Proximity Chat. Emotes and more player customisation options are also on the way, as well as a host of smaller features like replayable voice logs and more early-game accessible Biomods.

However, most fans will be desperate to hear what the next major update for Subnautica 2 has in store, and it shouldn’t disappoint. Gallegos explained that the update will be ‘progression and story-driven,’ whilst also offering an entirely new region to explore.

The said update will also provide a new vehicle, as well as a host of creatures and resources to uncover. To top things off, the new region is home to the now-revered Collector Leviathon, with it supposedly teeing up to be ‘the scariest the game has been yet.’

All of these updates and features sound incredibly enticing, and Subnautica 2 seems to be moving smoothly along its early access period. Release dates for these updates remain unknown, though the video description mentions they’ll arrive ‘over the coming weeks and months,’ so hopefully they won’t be too far away.

Are you excited for the future of Subnautica 2? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Helldivers 2’s new roguelite mode, as well as the release window for Horizon Steel Frontiers, the MMO set in the Horizon universe.

item.title: Helldivers 2 Roguelite Mode ‘Planet Warfronts’ Details Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Helldivers 2 developers have finally addressed their roguelite mode, dubbed Planet Warfronts.

For those unaware, towards the end of 2025, Arrowhead Game Studios piqued every player’s curiosity when the CCO mentioned they already had a prototype for a roguelite mode that would fundamentally change the game. Now, the details of this roguelite mode have been revealed through a new post on Steam.

Arrowhead Game Studios Discusses Helldivers 2 Roguelite Systems, Planet Warfronts

In a detailed blog post on Steam, the developers of Helldivers 2 finally addressed the roguelite systems they’ve been experimenting with, which they refer to as Planet Warfronts. Game Director Mikael Eriksson explained that, before the game’s launch, the team aimed to implement dynamic warfronts on various planets, requiring community decisions to liberate them. However, they did not have enough content to implement this concept to their satisfaction.

Now, the developers have enough content to do justice to Planet Warfronts and have grouped its missions across three categories. First is ‘Defend” missions in liberated territories, second is “Frontline War” missions on the frontlines, and the last one is Behind Enemy Lines” missions in enemy-controlled territory.

In Helldivers 2’s roguelite mode, called Planet Warfronts, players will battle to conquer key strategic locations on various planets, such as cities. These locations will feature different enemy variants, and players can expect gameplay-altering effects in the final version of this mode. Additionally, players will encounter consequences based on their progress toward planet liberation goals, such as liberating a city within a set time limit or disabling enemy artillery at a specific location to unlock essential Operations.

The developers aim to make planet liberation more fun with the new system and want to share one of their ideas currently in active development. More information will be shared in the next few months.

In other news, pre-production on the Helldivers movie starring Jason Momoa is set to begin, with the title scheduled for release in November 2027. What are your thoughts on the details of Helldivers 2’s roguelite mode, Planet Warfronts? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Horizon Steel Frontiers releasing in the first half of 2027. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
